text:
In a quiet little town nestled between a whispering forest and a shimmering lake, there lived a timid squirrel named Thimble. Thimble lived for nuts, though its favorite was the golden ones found under old maple trees. One autumn day, as Thimble was foraging for food under a blanket of gold leaves, it heard a peculiar chirping that didn’t sound like usual birds.

Curious, Thimble followed the sound to discover a tiny, shimmering creature perched on a pinecone. It flitted around, singing a melody so enchanting Thimble couldn’t resist.

This wasn’t your ordinary bird. It was a nightingale with feathers that glowed like stars when it sang. This morning song had Thimble enchanted.

That night, under a full moon, Thimble serenaded the night with a tune so beautiful that even stars seemed to brighten. The nightingale listened, its voice weaving through Thimble’s song
